title: ZREVRANGE
description: Returns members in a sorted set within a range of indexes in reverse order.
complexity: O(log(N)+M) with N being the number of elements in the sorted set and M the number of elements returned.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Returns the specified range of elements in the sorted set stored at `key`. The elements are considered to be ordered from the highest to the lowest score. Descending lexicographical order is used for elements with equal score. Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGE` is similar to [`ZRANGE`](). ## Examples ZADD myzset 1 "one" ZADD myzset 2 "two" ZADD myzset 3 "three" ZREVRANGE myzset 0 -1 ZREVRANGE myzset 2 3 ZREVRANGE myzset -2 -1 ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | Deprecated as of Redis v6.2.0. | ## Return information **RESP2:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified range, optionally with their scores if _WITHSCORE_ was used. **RESP3:** * [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified range, optionally with their scores if _WITHSCORE_ was used.
intro:
Returns the specified range of elements in the sorted set stored at `key`.
The elements are considered to be ordered from the highest to the lowest score.
Descending lexicographical order is used for elements with equal score.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGE` is similar to [`ZRANGE`]().

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Returns the specified range of elements in the sorted set stored at `key`.
The elements are considered to be ordered from the highest to the lowest score.
Descending lexicographical order is used for elements with equal score.

Apart from the reversed ordering, `ZREVRANGE` is similar to [`ZRANGE`]().

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
ZADD myzset 1 "one"
ZADD myzset 2 "two"
ZADD myzset 3 "three"
ZREVRANGE myzset 0 -1
ZREVRANGE myzset 2 3
ZREVRANGE myzset -2 -1

sections.title: Return information
sections.content:
**RESP2:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified range, optionally with their scores if _WITHSCORE_ was used.

**RESP3:**

* [Array reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#arrays): a list of members in the specified range, optionally with their scores if _WITHSCORE_ was used.
